Standard.NAME: Agent Transparency
Standard.purpose: Every agent action MUST declare its execution context. No action may exist outside of a defined skill, standard, framework, or tool.
Standard.principle: Transparency enables: - Traceability: know why agent did X - Auditability: verify agent followed rules - Learning: user understands agent behavior - Debugging: identify which layer caused issue

category.name: SKILL
category.description: Executing a defined skill from skills/ directory

category.name: STANDARD
category.description: Following a rule from standards/ directory

category.name: TOOL
category.description: Using external tool or MCP server

examples.example: OS detection, path validation, tool availability

rule.text: Before ANY action. agent MUST announce context
rule.example: [SKILL:vs-init] Reading vs.project.toml... [STANDARD:grace] Updating MODULE_CONTRACT in M-001... [TOOL:git] Committing changes...
rule.violation: Action without context prefix is a protocol violation

rule.text: Every action MUST belong to exactly ONE category
rule.valid: [SKILL:grace-status] Checking project health... [STANDARD:architecture] Validating layer dependencies...

rule.format: ✅ Done: [what was completed] ⏳ Next: [what comes next] 🔴 Blocked: [blocker] → [resolution needed]

rule.text: For complex operations. chain context with action
rule.example: [SKILL:vs-init] Step 3/5: Creating docs/ directory... └─→ [STANDARD:grace] Using development-plan.xml.template └─→ [TOOL:filesystem] Writing file

rule.text: When using MCP tools. prefix with mcp:
rule.example: [TOOL:mcp:conport] get_active_context → loading session... [TOOL:mcp:filesystem] read_file → loading config...
